title: Source
description: One verified data source. Lives in two places with the same shape: the durable registry sources/registry.json (deduplicated on normalized_url; consulted BEFORE any new web discovery so a re-run re-checks known sources instead of re-searching the web) and the per-run snapshot output/<location_id>/<run>/sources.json consumed by the Lead Scraper. Version 2.0 adds the registry fields (normalized_url, location_ids, first_seen, last_checked, last_result). Version 2.1 adds the change-detection fingerprint (content_hash, content_hash_at) so a scrape can skip re-extracting a page whose content has not changed.

id.description: Sequential id within a location run: SRC-001, SRC-002, ...

location_id.description: Which location this source belongs to.

entity.description: Public entity name, e.g. Round Rock ISD, City of Austin.

name.description: Short descriptive name, e.g. Purchasing / Bids (Bonfire).

contains.description: What the page actually contains, written from observation.

sport_confirmed.description: True only if a target sport (football, soccer, baseball/softball) is explicitly named.

validation_status.description: Result of the Phase 3 validation loop. Only verified sources are passed downstream.

validation_tier.description: Cumulative validation tiers the source cleared.

confidence.description: Reviewer sub-agent confidence score from the quality rubric.

reviewed.description: True once an independent reviewer sub-agent has scored it.

last_validated.description: UTC timestamp of the last successful validation.

normalized_url.description: Dedup key in the durable registry: lowercase scheme+host, fragment and tracking params stripped, no trailing slash. Two sources with the same normalized_url are the same source.

location_ids.description: All search areas this source covers (a state portal or aggregator can serve several). Superset of location_id.

first_seen.description: UTC timestamp when the source first entered the durable registry.

last_checked.description: UTC timestamp of the most recent re-check (validation or scrape), whatever its outcome.

last_result.description: Short note on the most recent check, e.g. 'verified; 3 leads in run 20260701T184158Z' or 'broken: 404'.

content_hash.description: Change-detection fingerprint: sha256 of the page body fetched with plain curl, whitespace-collapsed, recorded after the last SUCCESSFUL scrape of this source. A scrape run re-hashes the page first and skips extraction when the hash is unchanged. Absent/stale hash fails open (the source is scraped normally).

content_hash_at.description: UTC timestamp when content_hash was recorded (i.e. the last successful scrape whose extraction the hash represents).
